info.title: Stripe Sync Engine
info.description:
Stripe Sync Engine — stateless, one-shot source/destination sync over HTTP.
All sync endpoints accept configuration via a JSON request body.

## Endpoints

| Method | Path | Summary |
|--------|------|---------|
| GET | /health | Health check |
| POST | /pipeline_check | Check connector connection |
| POST | /pipeline_setup | Set up destination schema |
| POST | /pipeline_teardown | Tear down destination schema |
| POST | /source_discover | Discover available streams |
| POST | /pipeline_read | Read records from source |
| POST | /pipeline_write | Write records to destination |
| POST | /pipeline_sync | Run sync pipeline (read → write) |
| POST | /pipeline_sync_batch | Run sync pipeline (batch, returns JSON) |
| GET | /meta/sources | List available source connectors |
| GET | /meta/sources/{type} | Get source connector spec |
| GET | /meta/destinations | List available destination connectors |
| GET | /meta/destinations/{type} | Get destination connector spec |
| POST | /internal/query | Run a SQL query against a Postgres connection |

post.description: Validates the source/destination config and tests connectivity. Streams NDJSON messages (connection_status, log, trace) tagged with _emitted_by. Pass only=source or only=destination to check a single side.

only.description: Run only the source or destination side. Useful for optimistic destination setup or isolating a connector when debugging.

post.description: Creates destination tables and applies migrations. Streams NDJSON messages (control, log, trace) tagged with _emitted_by. Pass only=destination to run destination setup alone (e.g. optimistic table creation) or only=source to isolate the source.

only.description: Run only the source or destination side. Useful for optimistic destination setup or isolating a connector when debugging.

post.description: Drops destination tables. Streams NDJSON messages (log, trace) tagged with _emitted_by. Pass only=destination or only=source to run a single side.

only.description: Run only the source or destination side. Useful for optimistic destination setup or isolating a connector when debugging.

post.description: Streams NDJSON messages (catalog, logs, traces) for the configured source.

soft_time_limit.description: Soft wall-clock deadline in seconds. Stops reading from the source between messages; the destination continues to drain and flush until time_limit fires.

run_id.description: Optional sync run identifier used to track bounded sync progress.

stdin.description: Optional array of input messages (push mode). Without stdin, reads from the source connector (backfill mode).

state.description: SyncState ({ source, destination, sync_run }). Falls back to empty state if invalid.

post.description: Writes messages to the destination. Pass an array of messages in the request body.

stdin.description: Array of messages to write to the destination.

200.description: NDJSON stream of write result messages

post.description: Reads from the source connector and writes to the destination (backfill mode).

soft_time_limit.description: Soft wall-clock deadline in seconds. Stops reading from the source between messages; the destination continues to drain and flush until time_limit fires.

run_id.description: Optional sync run identifier used to track bounded sync progress.

stdin.description: Optional array of input messages (push mode). Without stdin, reads from the source connector (backfill mode).

state.description: SyncState ({ source, destination, sync_run }). Falls back to empty state if invalid.

post.description: Runs the full read → write pipeline and returns the final EofPayload as a single JSON response.

run_id.description: Optional sync run identifier used to track bounded sync progress.

state_limit.description: Stop after yielding N source_state messages, inclusive.

state.description: SyncState ({ source, destination, sync_run }). Falls back to empty state if invalid.

200.description: Available source connectors with their JSON Schema configs
Available destination connectors with their JSON Schema configs

post.summary: Run a SQL query against a Postgres connection

sync_mode.description: How the source reads this stream. Defaults to full_refresh.

fields.description: If set, only these fields are synced.

backfill_limit.description: Cap backfill to this many records, then mark the stream complete.

account_id.description: Stripe account ID (resolved from API if omitted)

account_created.description: Stripe account creation timestamp in unix seconds (resolved from API if omitted)

livemode.description: Whether this is a live mode sync

base_url.description: Override the Stripe API base URL (e.g. http://localhost:12111 for stripe-mock)

webhook_url.description: URL for managed webhook endpoint registration

webhook_secret.description: Webhook signing secret (whsec_...) for signature verification

websocket.description: Enable WebSocket streaming for live events

poll_events.description: Enable events API polling for incremental sync after backfill

webhook_port.description: Port for built-in webhook HTTP listener (e.g. 4242)

revalidate_objects.description: Object types to re-fetch from Stripe API on webhook (e.g. ["subscription"])

backfill_limit.description: Max objects to backfill per stream (useful for testing)

rate_limit.description: Override max requests per second (default: auto-derived from API key mode — 20 live, 10 test).

connection_string.description: Deprecated alias for url; prefer url

host.description: Postgres host for RDS IAM auth

port.description: Postgres port for RDS IAM auth

database.description: Database name for RDS IAM auth

user.description: Database user for RDS IAM auth

ssl_ca_pem.description: PEM-encoded CA certificate for SSL verification (required for verify-ca / verify-full with a private CA)

spreadsheet_title.description: Title when creating a new spreadsheet

batch_size.description: Rows per Sheets API append call

_emitted_by.description: Who emitted this message: "source/{type}", "destination/{type}", or "engine". Set by the engine.

_ts.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the engine observed this message.

stream.description: Stream (table) name this record belongs to.

data.description: The record payload as a key-value map.

emitted_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the record was emitted by the source.

_emitted_by.description: Who emitted this message: "source/{type}", "destination/{type}", or "engine". Set by the engine.

_ts.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the engine observed this message.

data.description: Opaque checkpoint data — only the source understands its contents. The orchestrator persists it keyed by stream and passes it back on resume.
Sync-wide state shared across all streams (e.g. a global events cursor).

anyOf.description: Sync-wide checkpoint shared across all streams.

_emitted_by.description: Who emitted this message: "source/{type}", "destination/{type}", or "engine". Set by the engine.

_ts.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the engine observed this message.

primary_key.description: Paths to fields that uniquely identify a record within this stream. Supports composite keys and nested paths. e.g. [["id"]] or [["account_id"], ["created"]]

json_schema.description: JSON Schema describing the record shape. Discovered at runtime or provided by config.

metadata.description: Source-specific metadata that applies to every record in this stream. The destination can use these for schema naming, partitioning, etc. Examples: Stripe: { api_version, account_id, live_mode }.

newer_than_field.description: Field whose value increases monotonically. Destination uses it to skip stale writes (e.g. "updated").

soft_delete_field.description: Field in record data that signals a soft delete (e.g. "deleted"). Destination uses this to classify upserts as deletes when the field is truthy.

items.description: A named collection of records — analogous to a table or API resource.

streams.description: All streams available from this source.

_emitted_by.description: Who emitted this message: "source/{type}", "destination/{type}", or "engine". Set by the engine.

_ts.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the engine observed this message.

data.description: Structured log fields emitted alongside the message.

log.description: Structured log output from a connector.

_emitted_by.description: Who emitted this message: "source/{type}", "destination/{type}", or "engine". Set by the engine.

_ts.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the engine observed this message.

config.description: JSON Schema for the connector's configuration object.

source_state_stream.description: JSON Schema for per-stream state (cursor/checkpoint shape). See also SourceState.global for sync-wide cursors.

source_input.description: JSON Schema for the read() input parameter (e.g. a webhook event).

soft_limit_fraction.description: Fraction of `time_limit` to use as default `soft_time_limit` (e.g. 0.5).

spec.description: JSON Schema describing the configuration a connector requires.

_emitted_by.description: Who emitted this message: "source/{type}", "destination/{type}", or "engine". Set by the engine.

_ts.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the engine observed this message.

message.description: Human-readable explanation of the check result.

_emitted_by.description: Who emitted this message: "source/{type}", "destination/{type}", or "engine". Set by the engine.

_ts.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the engine observed this message.

time_range.description: Full backfill time span for this stream.

stream_status.description: Stream lifecycle event. Sources emit these; the engine tracks stream progress from them.

_emitted_by.description: Who emitted this message: "source/{type}", "destination/{type}", or "engine". Set by the engine.

_ts.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the engine observed this message.

control.description: Control signal from a connector to the orchestrator.

_emitted_by.description: Who emitted this message: "source/{type}", "destination/{type}", or "engine". Set by the engine.

_ts.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the engine observed this message.

started_at.description: When this sync started (ISO 8601); generally equals time_ceiling.

elapsed_ms.description: Wall-clock milliseconds since the sync run started.

global_state_count.description: Total source_state messages observed so far.

connection_status.description: Set when source or destination emits connection_status: failed.

message.description: Human-readable explanation of the check result.

records_per_second.description: Overall throughput for the entire run.

total_state_count.description: Total source_state messages across all streams.

streams.description: Per-stream progress, keyed by stream name.

ProgressPayload.description: Periodic sync progress emitted by the engine as a top-level message. Each emission is a full replacement.

RunStatus.description: succeeded = all streams completed/skipped; failed = connection_status failed OR any stream errored.

status.description: Current state, derived from stream_status events.

state_count.description: Number of state checkpoints for this stream.

record_count.description: Records synced for this stream in this run.

message.description: Human-readable status message (error reason, skip reason, etc).

total_range.description: Full backfill time span for this stream.

completed_ranges.description: Completed time sub-ranges within the total_range.

_emitted_by.description: Who emitted this message: "source/{type}", "destination/{type}", or "engine". Set by the engine.

_ts.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the engine observed this message.

status.description: Terminal run status derived from stream outcomes.

has_more.description: Whether the client should continue with another request. true when cut off by limits; false when the source iterator exhausted naturally.

ending_state.description: Full sync state at the end of this request. Round-trip this as starting_state on the next request.

run_progress.description: Accumulated progress across all requests in this sync run.

request_progress.description: Progress for this specific request only.

EofPayload.description: Terminal message signaling end of this request.

run_id.description: Identifies a finite backfill run. Omit for continuous sync.

time_ceiling.description: Frozen upper bound (ISO 8601). Set on first invocation when run_id is present; reused on continuation.

progress.description: Accumulated progress from prior requests in this run.

sync_run.description: Engine-managed run state — run_id, time_ceiling, accumulated progress.

SyncState.description: Full sync checkpoint with separate sections for source, destination, and sync run. Connectors only see their own section; the engine manages routing.

streams.description: Per-stream checkpoint data, keyed by stream name.

global.description: Source-wide state shared across all streams.

SourceState.description: Source connector state — cursors, backfill progress, events cursors.

_emitted_by.description: Who emitted this message: "source/{type}", "destination/{type}", or "engine". Set by the engine.

_ts.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the engine observed this message.
